name: Madison County v. Oneida Indian Nation
question:
(1)Does tribal sovereign immunity from suit prevent taxing authorities from foreclosing to collect lawfully imposed property taxes?

(2) Is the ancient Oneida reservation in New York disestablished or diminished?
description: A case which the Court vacated the judgement and remanded the case back to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit for further proceedings, because the Onieda Nation waived its sovereign immunity.
